mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of setup for distributed environments.

steps.title: Install the vLLM CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface, which is necessary for managing vLLM deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new GKE cluster with the specified version and node count, which is required to run vLLM.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys vLLM using Helm, which simplifies the management of Kubernetes applications.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in the vllm namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the vLLM release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade vLLM to the latest version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the size of the existing nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations as needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or is not running.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the vLLM pods are running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved PagedAttention performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' with nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
